trade.summary: The US and France are major trading partners. France exports aircraft (Airbus), luxury goods, pharmaceuticals, and wine to the US. The US exports aerospace, pharmaceuticals, energy, and IT services to France. Trade has been periodically disrupted by tariff disputes (Boeing-Airbus WTO saga), but overall volumes are large and growing.
disputes: US tariffs on French wine and cheese (2019-2021)

diplomatic.summary: France and the US share the world's oldest alliance, but relations are often turbulent. The AUKUS submarine deal (2021) — which cut France out of a lucrative contract with Australia — was a major rupture, described by France as a 'betrayal.' Relations recovered under the Biden administration. Under Trump's second term (2025), French concerns about US commitment to NATO and Ukraine have re-emerged, intensifying Macron's push for European strategic autonomy.
recent_incidents: US under Trump questioning NATO commitments (2025)
France-US divergence on Ukraine peace terms (2025)

military.summary: The Franco-American alliance is the oldest in US history. Both are NATO's two largest non-US Western military powers. France re-joined NATO's integrated military command in 2009. Both share significant intelligence cooperation (SIGINT, counterterrorism). France was a key partner in counterterrorism operations in the Sahel and Middle East. Under Trump 2.0, France has elevated European defence autonomy discussions.

financial.summary: Massive bilateral investment. The US and France are among each other's largest foreign investors. US tech giants (Google, Microsoft, Apple) have major French operations. French multinationals (LVMH, TotalEnergies, BNP Paribas, Airbus) are significant US employers. Financial ties are the deepest of any two non-anglophone bilateral pair.

energy.summary: The US has become a major LNG supplier to France, replacing Russian gas post-2022. France's nuclear power industry purchases some uranium from US sources. Cooperation in nuclear power technology and clean energy innovation through bilateral agreements.

scientific.summary: One of the world's deepest bilateral science and technology partnerships. Hundreds of joint research programmes at university and government level. CNRS-US collaborations span every scientific domain. French and American researchers co-author extensively. Competition in AI and semiconductor ecosystems coexists with deep collaboration.
